about.title: About AGROBUSINESS
about.subtitle: A platform built to connect the entire Brazilian agribusiness chain.
about.p1: AGROBUSINESS was born to give rural producers, buyers, stores and agri brands a single place to meet, negotiate and grow together.
about.p2: We bring together marketplace, prices, weather, news, courses and a benefits club in a simple, mobile-first experience made for those who live the field.
about.p3: We are committed to transparency, information security and respect for privacy law — so every user has control over their data and negotiations.

mission.title: Mission
mission.desc: Connect Brazilian agribusiness with technology, transparency and simplicity.

vision.title: Vision
vision.desc: Be the leading Brazilian agri platform, from field to industry.

values.title: Values
values.desc: Trust, closeness to the producer and continuous evolution.

legal.draftNotice: This text is a draft and must be replaced by the official legal content provided by the responsible attorney. It does not constitute legal advice.

s1.title: 1. Acceptance
s1.body: By using AGROBUSINESS you declare that you have read and agreed to these Terms of Use. If you do not agree, do not use the platform.

s2.title: 2. Account & sign-up
s2.body: The user is responsible for the information provided at sign-up and for keeping access credentials confidential.

s3.title: 3. Use of the platform
s3.body: Using the service for unlawful, fraudulent, offensive purposes or in violation of third-party rights is prohibited.

s4.title: 4. Listings & negotiations
s4.body: Negotiations between users are the responsibility of the parties involved. AGROBUSINESS acts as a connection intermediary.

s5.title: 5. Intellectual property
s5.body: All content, brand and software of AGROBUSINESS is protected by copyright and other applicable laws.

s6.title: 6. Changes
s6.body: These Terms may be updated at any time. Please review this page periodically. (Draft — replace with the official legal text.)

s1.title: 1. What data we collect
s1.body: We collect data provided at sign-up (name, email, phone, location, categories of interest) and usage data, in compliance with privacy law.

s2.title: 2. How we use the data
s2.body: We use data to provide the service, personalize the experience, ensure security and comply with legal obligations.

s3.title: 3. Sharing
s3.body: We do not sell personal data. We share only with providers essential to platform operation and where legally required.

s4.title: 4. User rights
s4.body: You can request access, correction, portability or deletion of your data at any time, as per privacy law.

s5.title: 5. Security
s5.body: We adopt technical and organizational measures to protect data against unauthorized access, loss or improper alteration.

s6.title: 6. Contact
s6.body: For questions about this policy, contact us via the official support channel. (Draft — replace with the official legal text.)

negociar.subtitle: Chat with prospects and track every deal.

painel.title: The Dashboard: your starting point
painel.summary: The Dashboard brings the essentials of your day to one screen: greeting, featured listings, dollar quotes and business KPIs.
steps: Open 'Dashboard' in the menu (first option).
Check the 'Featured' carousel below the greeting: it shows the most visible listings right now.
See the dollar cards (commercial, tourism, parallel) at the top — the shown types are configurable.
Scroll to 'My business' for your KPIs: listed products, volume, messages and negotiations.
painel.example: You open the app in the morning, notice the commercial dollar rose 0.8%, and see 3 new messages on your soy listing.
painel.tip: Click any KPI card to jump straight to the related screen.

navegacao.title: How the menu is organized
navegacao.summary: Where to find each thing in the app's side menu.
steps: BUSINESS: buy, your listings (sell), negotiations, feature a listing and messages.
SERVICES CHANNEL: service areas like Logistics, Consulting, Financing, Learning, Startups and Export/Import. Some items open the Buy page already filtered by that category.
BENEFITS CLUB: perks like Insurance, Machinery and Inputs.
MARKET: quotes, news and price alerts. LEARNING: courses and certificates. ACCOUNT: your profile, plan, Club and settings.
Items tagged 'Coming soon' will launch later — they already appear so you know what's on the way.
navegacao.example: Need to hire freight? Go to Services Channel → Logistics: it opens Buy already filtered by Transporte e Logística.
navegacao.tip: 'Coming soon' items don't open anything yet — it's just a heads-up about what's coming.

conta-e-plano.title: Your account and plan
conta-e-plano.summary: 'My account' lets you edit your data; 'Plans' lets you pick Free or Pro with more features.
steps: In the menu, open 'Account' > 'My account'.
Update your name, phone and photo whenever needed.
To subscribe or switch plans, go to 'Account > Plans' and choose Pro.
To cancel or change card, click 'Manage subscription' on the same page.
conta-e-plano.example: Free lets you browse and publish a few listings; Pro unlocks Kanban negotiations, finance, reports and more highlights.
conta-e-plano.tip: A lock icon next to a menu item means it's Pro-only.

minha-localizacao.title: My location (via ZIP)
minha-localizacao.summary: Setting your location turns on the 'near me' filter and helps the app show nearby CDs and listings.
steps: Go to 'Account > My account' and find 'My location'.
Type the ZIP: city, state and coordinates are filled automatically.
If you prefer, tweak city and state by hand.
Save. The distance filter now works on Buy.
minha-localizacao.example: After setting a ZIP in Ribeirão Preto, the app sorts listings with CDs within 100 km of you.
minha-localizacao.tip: Without coordinates the distance filter is off — the app warns you at the top of Buy.

preferencias.title: Preferences: language, currency and visuals
preferencias.summary: Personalize the app: language (PT/EN/ES), preferred currency, which dollars appear and the carousel speed.
steps: Open 'Account > Settings'.
Pick a language at the top (PT, EN or ES).
Select your preferred currency (BRL, USD or EUR) and default dollar type.
Adjust the highlights carousel timing (2 to 15 seconds).
preferencias.example: An exporter picks USD as preferred currency and starts seeing every price converted automatically.
preferencias.tip: The dollar chips atop the Quotes page also control which dollars show on the Dashboard.

comprar.title: Buy: find offers
comprar.summary: Buy lists open ads with filters by category, state, quality, delivery, certification, barter and price range. Prices show in your preferred currency.
steps: In the menu, open 'Business > Buy'.
Use the top search bar to type the product.
Open the filters and refine by category (cascading catalog), state, quality, accepts barter, certification, etc.
In the min and max price fields, use YOUR display currency — the app converts each listing automatically for comparison.
Tap a card to see details, photos and chat with the seller.
comprar.example: You look for 'arabica coffee' in MG, filter by organic certification and find three offers with direct farmer contact.
comprar.tip: Price sorting already takes currency conversion into account, so BRL, USD and EUR listings are compared fairly.

perto-de-mim.title: Near me (distance radius)
perto-de-mim.summary: With your location set, you can list only ads whose Distribution Center is within a radius.
steps: Confirm your location in 'Account > My account'.
On Buy, turn on 'Near me' and pick a radius (e.g., 50, 100, 200 km).
The list now shows only offers with CDs inside the radius, sorted by distance.
perto-de-mim.example: A buyer in Uberlândia (MG) uses a 150 km radius and only sees listings with pickup at nearby CDs.
perto-de-mim.tip: If 'no coordinates' appears, tap the notice and set your ZIP in one click.

criar-anuncio.title: Publish a listing
criar-anuncio.summary: Publish a product or service in a few steps: pick the catalog, set unit, price (in the currency you want to be paid) and details.
steps: In the menu, open 'Business > My Ads' and click 'New listing'.
Pick the type (product or service) and browse the catalog to the right category.
Fill in title, description, unit, quantity and price.
Pick the price CURRENCY (Real, US Dollar or Euro) — it is the currency you want to receive.
Add photos (drag and drop), mark certifications and whether you accept barter.
To reach buyers abroad, turn on 'Available for export' and (optionally) pick the incoterm and destination countries.
If you have a CD, link it to the listing. Save and you're done.
criar-anuncio.example: A farmer publishes 'Bulk corn', 500 bags, R$ 60.00 each, with a photo of the lot and a link to the Rondonópolis CD.
criar-anuncio.tip: Good photos and the right category greatly boost views on your ad.

meus-anuncios.title: Manage My Ads
meus-anuncios.summary: On 'My Ads' you can edit, pause/resume, mark as sold, delete and access per-listing inventory.
steps: Open 'Business > My Ads'.
Each card has 'Edit', 'Inventory' (products only) and 'Highlight' buttons.
The '⋯' menu holds 'Pause', 'Mark as sold' and 'Delete'.
'Pause' hides the ad from Buy without deleting; resume any time.
meus-anuncios.example: You sold half the lot off-app and pause the ad until you restock.
meus-anuncios.tip: 'Mark as sold' preserves history and helps with future reports.

destaque.title: Highlight: get to the top
destaque.summary: Highlight puts your ad in the Dashboard carousel, on top of listings, and adds a golden badge.
steps: Go to 'Business > Highlight ad' (or click 'Highlight' on a card).
Pick a package (e.g., 7, 15 or 30 days).
Complete payment; your ad gets the badge automatically.
Track the expiry date right on the ad card.
destaque.example: A farmer highlights for 15 days during soy harvest and triples the ad's views.
destaque.tip: Great for harvest peaks and dates when demand rises.

startups-pmes.title: Startups & SMBs
startups-pmes.summary: A dedicated channel for service providers and agri startups. Ads here don't show on regular Buy unless highlighted.
steps: Mark your profile as 'Startup/SMB' in 'My account'.
Publish through the 'Startups & SMBs' module — services only.
To appear on general Buy too, hire a Highlight.
startups-pmes.example: An AgTech publishes a per-hectare soil analysis service; the 'Startup' badge appears on the card.
startups-pmes.tip: Startups sell better with a fresh institutional page and portfolio photos in the ad.

o-que-e-cd.title: What is a Distribution Center (CD)
o-que-e-cd.summary: A CD is a warehouse that receives, stores and redistributes goods. In the app, it links ads to physical pickup locations.
steps: Each CD has address, capacity and a responsible person.
Ads can be linked to one or several CDs.
Buyers can filter by CD and by distance to you.
o-que-e-cd.example: A cooperative registers its CD in Sorriso (MT); its 12 ads become linked and visible by proximity.
o-que-e-cd.tip: A well-registered CD (with ZIP and coordinates) is the base of the 'near me' filter.

cadastrar-meu-cd.title: Register my own CD
cadastrar-meu-cd.summary: Anyone offering 'Transport/Logistics' or 'Storage' can register a CD; it stays pending until admin approval.
steps: Go to 'Account > My account' and click 'Become a Distribution Center'.
Fill name, contact person, phone, address and ZIP (the app fills city/state and coordinates).
Submit. The CD sits as 'Pending approval'.
Once approved, the 'My CDs' menu appears and you can receive links and record inventory.
cadastrar-meu-cd.example: A carrier registers two CDs (Uberaba and Anápolis); within one business day both are approved and it starts operating.
cadastrar-meu-cd.tip: You can also start registration when picking a logistics category in the listing form.

vincular-cd.title: Link a CD to your listing
vincular-cd.summary: Linking a CD tells where the product ships from. It feeds the distance filter and enables per-CD inventory.
steps: Edit your listing in 'My Ads'.
In the 'Distribution Centers' section, pick one or more approved CDs (or your own pending ones).
Save. Each linked CD now has its own inventory balance.
vincular-cd.example: A coffee product is linked to two CDs; each has its own independent stock.
vincular-cd.tip: If the buyer wants pickup in a certain city, they'll find you faster when you link that region's CD.

estoque.title: Per-CD inventory control
estoque.summary: For each ad-CD, you record entries and exits. The balance updates in real time and the app won't let you sell more than the balance.
steps: In 'My Ads', click 'Inventory' on the product card.
Pick the CD and set type (entry/exit), quantity and an optional note.
The balance shows at the top; full history sits below.
An exit larger than balance is blocked with a warning.
estoque.example: You post an entry of 200 bags at harvest and later post exits as sales close.
estoque.tip: A note (e.g., 'sale to John, invoice 123') helps a lot at end-of-month reconciliation.

mensagens.title: Messages: chat with prospects
mensagens.summary: The in-app chat lets you negotiate without sharing phone numbers. Each ad can spawn a conversation.
steps: Click 'Chat with seller' on an ad, or open 'Messages' in the menu.
Type your question; the other party gets a notification.
Turn on auto-translation in the chat if you speak a different language.
mensagens.example: An Argentinian buyer asks in Spanish; the farmer reads it in Portuguese with the translation toggle on.
mensagens.tip: Agree on price and terms in chat and move the card to 'In negotiation' on the Kanban.

negociacoes-kanban.title: Kanban negotiations
negociacoes-kanban.summary: Drag cards across columns to track each deal: started → in negotiation → closed (or discarded).
steps: Open 'Business > Negotiations' (Pro plan).
Drag a card with the mouse (or long-press on mobile) to change columns.
The card's status updates automatically on drop.
negociacoes-kanban.example: You drag the soy deal to 'Closed' when the payment lands; the card color changes.
negociacoes-kanban.tip: Use 'Discarded' so you don't lose contacts that didn't move forward.

cotacoes.title: Quotes: commodities and dollar
cotacoes.summary: Follow agri reference prices and dollar quotes (commercial, tourism, parallel).
steps: Open 'Market > Quotes'.
In the top chips, choose which dollar types you want to see.
Each card shows current value, change (%) and a mini chart of the last days.
Green = up; red = down.
cotacoes.example: You notice live cattle rose 1.2% in a week and decide to hold the sale a few more days.
cotacoes.tip: The chips picked here also control what shows on the Dashboard.

alertas-preco.title: Price alerts
alertas-preco.summary: Get a notification when a commodity price crosses the value you defined.
steps: Open 'Market > Alerts' and click 'New alert'.
Choose the commodity, the condition (above/below) and the target price.
When it triggers, the app pings you via the notification bell.
alertas-preco.example: 'Soy above R$ 145.00 per bag' fires and you get an instant notification.
alertas-preco.tip: Start with a few well-picked alerts so they don't become daily noise.

clima.title: Weather: track several cities
clima.summary: Add the cities relevant to your operation and see their weather on the Dashboard.
steps: Go to 'Account > Settings' and find 'Weather locations'.
Add a city by typing its name and picking from the list.
Remove cities when no longer needed.
Cities show in a carousel on the mobile Dashboard.
clima.example: A farmer with fields in MT and PR tracks both cities at the same time without leaving the app.
clima.tip: Keep up to 5 cities for a quick, clean read.

noticias.title: Agri news
noticias.summary: See curated headlines and pick the topics you want to follow (multi-select).
steps: Open 'Market > News'.
Tap topics to toggle on/off (grains, livestock, inputs, weather, etc.).
The list adjusts automatically to your picks.
noticias.example: Turning on just 'Grains' and 'Exports' keeps you focused on what's useful to your business.
noticias.tip: Each headline opens the original source in a new tab, without leaving the app.

cursos-certificados.title: Courses and Certificates
cursos-certificados.summary: Watch classes, track progress and, upon completion, issue a certificate with a validation code.
steps: Open 'Learning > Courses' and pick a track.
Mark each class as done to update progress.
When finished, generate the certificate under 'Learning > Certificates'.
Share the link: anyone can check the code on the public page.
cursos-certificados.example: You finish 'Farm Cost Management' and send the certificate link to a buyer who asked for proof.
cursos-certificados.tip: The certificate code is unique and can be verified without login.

clube-vantagens.title: Perks Club (Pro)
clube-vantagens.summary: Partner offers and discounts for Pro subscribers.
steps: Open 'Account > Perks Club' in the menu — or click the 'Perks Club' card inside the 'Account' page.
If your plan doesn't include the Club, an upgrade option to Pro shows up on the same screen.
Filter by category to find the offer you want.
Copy the coupon and click 'Redeem' to open the partner site.
clube-vantagens.example: A Pro subscriber applies a 15% coupon on farm inputs directly on the partner site.
clube-vantagens.tip: Watch the expiry — some offers change every month.

moedas.title: How currency works in the app
moedas.summary: When creating a listing, YOU pick the price currency (Real, US Dollar or Euro). Whoever sees it reads the price converted to the currency THEY chose in preferences.
steps: When creating or editing a listing, pick the price currency (BRL, USD or EUR).
Type the price in that currency — it's what you want to be paid.
In 'Account > Settings', pick YOUR display currency.
Every price now shows in it, converted automatically using the day's rates.
moedas.example: You list soy at US$ 20 per bag. A buyer who prefers Real sees the value already converted to R$.
moedas.tip: The listing currency is what you receive; the display currency is just how each user sees it. If a rate is missing, the price shows in the original currency — it's never converted wrong.

exportacao.title: Mark your listing for export
exportacao.summary: The listing form has an 'Available for export' toggle. Turning it on adds an 'Export' badge and also lists it on the International page, without leaving the local Buy.
steps: Open 'Business > My Ads' and edit (or create) a listing.
Turn on the 'Available for export' toggle.
Optional: pick an Incoterm (international delivery rule) and the destination countries you serve.
Save. The 'Export' badge appears on the card automatically.
exportacao.example: You mark a coffee lot as 'Available for export', pick FOB and add Germany and Italy as destinations. The listing stays on Buy and also shows up for international buyers.
exportacao.tip: You don't lose the domestic market — the listing keeps showing on the local Buy.

canal-internacional.title: The International page
canal-internacional.summary: Under 'Business' there is 'International': a showcase with only the listings marked as available for export.
steps: Open 'Business > International' in the menu.
Use the 'Destination country' filter to see only sellers who serve the country you chose.
Tap a card to see details, Incoterm, destinations and chat with the seller.
canal-internacional.example: A buyer in Portugal filters by 'Portugal' and sees only listings whose sellers marked that country as a destination.
canal-internacional.tip: If a listing doesn't show up here, the seller probably hasn't turned on the 'Available for export' toggle.

incoterms.title: Incoterms: understand in 1 minute
incoterms.summary: An Incoterm is the international rule that sets how far the seller's responsibility goes (freight, insurance, customs). It's optional and lets the buyer know the deal up front.
steps: When you turn on 'Available for export', pick the Incoterm from the selector.
The Incoterm appears on the card and on the listing detail page.
If you don't pick one, the listing goes as 'to be agreed'.
incoterms.example: The three most common in agri: EXW — buyer picks up at origin; FOB — seller delivers on board the ship (port of loading); CIF — seller pays freight and insurance to the port of destination.
incoterms.tip: In grains, when in doubt, FOB and CIF are the most common. Confirm it in chat before closing.

meu-pais.title: Set your country
meu-pais.summary: In 'Account' you set your country. It helps buyers abroad find you and places the app in the international market. Country names show up automatically in your language.
steps: Open 'Account > My account'.
Find the 'My country' field and pick your country from the list.
Save. Done — the info is already used on international screens.
meu-pais.example: A Brazilian exporter sets 'Brazil' as country; European buyers can identify the seller's origin on each listing.
meu-pais.tip: If you change the app language, country names translate automatically — no need to set it again.

pricing.rateUnavailable: exchange rate unavailable — showing in the listing currency

international.title: Exports
international.subtitle: Listings available to international buyers.
international.formToggleDesc: Enable to offer this listing to international buyers.
international.countriesHint: Select one or more countries you can export to.
international.myCountryDesc: Used to identify international buyers and improve recommendations.
international.setYourCountryHint: Set your country in Account for a full international experience.
